instructions.instruction: Before choosing a pattern, state the concrete problem without pattern jargon: what conditional logic or coupling exists, what needs to vary independently, and what external system needs integration.
instructions.relevant_when: Evaluating a codebase to decide whether a design pattern applies
instructions.why_given: Step 1 of the workflow requires a concrete problem statement before pattern selection

instructions.instruction: When proposing a pattern, explicitly state the win condition: what complexity the pattern removes and what it adds. If the cost outweighs the benefit for simple code, recommend staying with the current approach.
instructions.relevant_when: Recommending or applying a design pattern
instructions.why_given: Step 2 requires evaluating pattern fit with an explicit win condition and cost/benefit analysis

instructions.instruction: Use the Strategy pattern to eliminate if/else chains or switch statements that select between interchangeable algorithms. Extract each branch into a class implementing a common interface.
instructions.relevant_when: Code contains conditional logic that selects between algorithm variants
instructions.why_given: Strategy pattern section: 'Use when: Conditional logic selects between algorithms'

instructions.instruction: Use the Adapter pattern when integrating an external or legacy API whose interface does not match your domain interface. Create a thin adapter class that translates between the two interfaces.
instructions.relevant_when: Integrating external APIs or legacy systems with incompatible interfaces
instructions.why_given: Adapter pattern section: 'Use when: Integrating external APIs or legacy systems with incompatible interfaces'

instructions.instruction: Use the Anti-Corruption Layer pattern to prevent external system design from polluting domain models. The ACL translates external data types to domain types and must live at the infrastructure boundary.
instructions.relevant_when: Integrating with external systems that have poor or incompatible domain models
instructions.why_given: Anti-Corruption Layer section prescribes this for preventing external pollution of the domain

instructions.instruction: NEVER apply patterns preemptively for imagined future needs. Apply a Factory or Strategy only when a second concrete implementation exists or complexity justifies it.
instructions.relevant_when: Evaluating whether to extract a pattern when only one implementation exists
instructions.why_given: Anti-pattern: NEVER apply patterns preemptively — wait for concrete need
